data.title: Skill Entry-Point UX: Smart Routing and Friction Reduction
data.description:
## Context
When DSO practitioners start their work session — invoking /dso:brainstorm or /dso:sprint with no arguments — the entry-point experience introduces unnecessary friction at every turn. Brainstorm opens a blank "What feature are you building?" prompt even when underdeveloped epics already exist. Sprint shows all epics including those with zero children that aren't ready for execution. Sprint also blocks on a validate.sh --ci gate that adds wall-clock time before a practitioner can begin. And brainstorm's gap analysis batches multiple questions in a single message, violating its own one-question-at-a-time guardrail. All four issues share the same root cause: the skill entry points were designed for the happy path and lack intelligent defaults that guide practitioners to the right next action.

## Success Criteria
1. When /dso:brainstorm is invoked with no arguments, it displays a selectable list of open, unblocked epics with 0 children, plus a "start fresh" option — and shows a count of epics with >0 children below the list
2. When no 0-child epics exist, brainstorm automatically falls through to the "start fresh" dialogue
3. When /dso:sprint is invoked with no arguments, it only displays epics with >=1 child in the selectable list — and shows a count of hidden 0-child epics below the list with a suggestion to run /dso:brainstorm
4. When sprint filters result in no eligible epics (all have 0 children), sprint suggests running /dso:brainstorm and exits
5. The validate.sh --ci pre-execution gate is removed from sprint Phase 1
6. Brainstorm's gap analysis (Phase 1 Gate Step 2) asks one question at a time instead of batching up to 3
7. After delivery, run 3 real practitioner sessions end-to-end and confirm: (a) no blank-prompt or wrong-epic-in-list issues appear, (b) sprint starts within 5 seconds of epic selection (no validation gate delay), and (c) gap analysis never presents more than one question per message

## Scenario Analysis
- --max-children=0 interpreted as unset/falsy in bash (critical, Configuration): The flag parser must use set-check (${max_children+x}) rather than truthiness test to handle zero correctly
- sprint-list-epics.sh flag parsing breaks existing callers (high, Runtime): New flags must be added with proper argument parsing that does not consume existing positional args
- Archived epics with 0 children appear in brainstorm list (high, Configuration): sprint-list-epics.sh must explicitly exclude archived epics when filtering by child count
- check-shim-refs.sh blocks commit if SKILL.md references scripts directly (high, Deployment): SKILL.md edits must use .claude/scripts/dso shim, not direct plugins/dso/scripts/ paths

## Approach
Add --min-children=N and --max-children=N flags to sprint-list-epics.sh for script-level filtering. Update brainstorm SKILL.md to call the script with --max-children=0 for the no-args path. Update sprint SKILL.md to call with --min-children=1. Remove the validation gate section from sprint. Modify brainstorm's gap analysis to use sequential one-at-a-time questioning.

## Dependencies
None

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 4
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
